targets.name: Render
targets.cost: Free tier: 750 hrs/mo, 512MB RAM, sleeps after 15min idle

DATABASE_URL.hint: Neon connection string or empty (seed.json fallback)

targets.db_option: Neon (Render Blueprint → add PostgreSQL sidecar or external Neon)
targets.deploy_method: GitHub repo connect → Auto-deploy on push → SSH auth: Render manages deploy keys
targets.notes: Best first choice. Dockerfile ready. No DB = seed.json mode.

targets.name: Fly.io
targets.cost: Free tier: 3 shared-cpu-1x 256MB VMs, 3GB persisted volume (for SQLite)

DATABASE_URL.hint: Neon or SQLite path like sqlite:///data/shinjuku_lunch.db

targets.db_option: SQLite on persisted volume (3GB free) or Neon external
targets.deploy_method: flyctl launch → GitHub Actions (or manual fly deploy) → SSH via flyctl auth
targets.notes: Best if you want SQLite in production. Volume persists across redeploys.

targets.name: Railway
targets.cost: Free tier: $5 credit/mo (roughly 500 hrs), no sleep
targets.db_option: Railway PostgreSQL plugin (free with limits) or external Neon
targets.deploy_method: GitHub repo connect → Auto-deploy → SSH: Railway handles deploy keys
targets.notes: Good middle ground. No sleep means always-on.

targets.name: Google Cloud Run
targets.db_option: Neon only (ephemeral filesystem, no SQLite persistence)
targets.deploy_method: gcloud CLI or Cloud Build GitHub trigger → SSH not needed
targets.notes: Only viable with Neon. Best for production at scale.

recommendation.reason: Dockerfile ready, free tier sufficient, zero-config GitHub deploy

ssh_setup.shared_pc_note: 共有PCなので ssh-keygen にパスフレーズを設定し、エージェントのタイムアウトを短めに。または GitHub CLI (gh auth login) でブラウザ認証が安全。

options.method: gh auth login (Recommended for shared PC)
options.note: ブラウザ認証。ログアウトも簡単 (gh auth logout)。キーをPCに残さない。
パスフレーズ必須。エージェントに ssh-add -t 3600 で1h制限。

todo: Choose target → create account → connect GitHub repo
Set DATABASE_URL env var (or leave empty for seed.json)
If using seed.json: update Dockerfile to COPY --from=... neon/seed.json or embed via embed.FS
If using SQLite on Fly.io: add fly.toml with [mounts] and SQLite volume
